apps.app_name_desc: Application Name, as shown on Moonlight
apps.applications_desc: Applications are refreshed only when Client is restarted
apps.auto_detach: Continue streaming if the application exits quickly
apps.auto_detach_desc: This will attempt to automatically detect launcher-type apps that close quickly after launching another program or instance of themselves. When a launcher-type app is detected, it is treated as a detached app.
apps.cmd_desc: The main application to start. If blank, no application will be started.
apps.cmd_note: If the path to the command executable contains spaces, you must enclose it in quotes.
apps.cmd_prep_desc: A list of commands to be run before/after this application. If any of the prep-commands fail, starting the application is aborted.
apps.detached_cmds_desc: A list of commands to be run in the background.
apps.detached_cmds_note: If the path to the command executable contains spaces, you must enclose it in quotes.
apps.env_client_audio_config: The Audio Configuration requested by the client (2.0/5.1/7.1)
apps.env_client_enable_sops: The client has requested the option to optimize the game for optimal streaming (true/false)
apps.env_client_fps: The FPS requested by the client (int, multiplied by 1000)
apps.env_client_gcmap: The requested gamepad mask, in a bitset/bitfield format (int)
apps.env_client_hdr: HDR is enabled by the client (true/false)
apps.env_client_height: The Height requested by the client (int)
apps.env_client_host_audio: The client has requested host audio (true/false)
apps.env_client_width: The Width requested by the client (int)
apps.env_vars_desc: All commands get these environment variables by default:
apps.exit_timeout_desc: Number of seconds to wait for all app processes to gracefully exit when requested to quit. If unset, the default is to wait up to 5 seconds. If set to 0, the app will be immediately terminated.
apps.global_prep_desc: Enable/Disable the execution of Global Prep Commands for this application.
apps.image_desc: Application icon/picture/image path that will be sent to client. Image must be a PNG file. If not set, Vibepollo will send default box image.
apps.name: Name
apps.output_desc: The file where the output of the command is stored, if it is not specified, the output is ignored
apps.run_as_desc: This can be necessary for some applications that require administrator permissions to run properly.
apps.wait_all: Continue streaming until all app processes exit
apps.wait_all_desc: This will continue streaming until all processes started by the app have terminated. When unchecked, streaming will stop when the initial app process exits, even if other app processes are still running.
apps.confirm_delete_message_named: This removes the application entry "{name}" from your list. It does not delete files on disk.
apps.delete_autosync_body: This removes every Playnite-managed auto-sync entry from Applications. Manual entries are not affected.
apps.delete_autosync_error: Failed to delete auto-synced Playnite games.
apps.cannot_delete_autosync: Auto-synced Playnite app cannot be deleted.
apps.playnite_edit_notice: This application is managed by Playnite. Command, Working Directory, and Detached commands are ignored. Prep commands still run before and after the game.
apps.working_dir_desc: The working directory that should be passed to the process. For example, some applications use the working directory to search for configuration files. If not set, Vibepollo will default to the parent directory of the command

config.adapter_name_desc_linux_1: Manually specify a GPU to use for capture.
config.adapter_name_desc_linux_2: to find all devices capable of VAAPI
config.adapter_name_desc_linux_3: Replace ``renderD129`` with the device from above to lists the name and capabilities of the device. To be supported by Vibepollo, it needs to have at the very minimum:
config.adapter_name_desc_windows: Manually specify a GPU to use for capture. If unset, the GPU is chosen automatically. We strongly recommend leaving this field blank to use automatic GPU selection! Note: This GPU must have a display connected and powered on. The appropriate values can be found using the following command:
config.address_family_desc: Set the address family used by Vibepollo
config.always_send_scancodes_desc: Sending scancodes enhances compatibility with games and apps but may result in incorrect keyboard input from certain clients that aren't using a US English keyboard layout. Enable if keyboard input is not working at all in certain applications. Disable if keys on the client are generating the wrong input on the host.
config.amd_coder_desc: Allows you to select the entropy encoding to prioritize quality or encoding speed. H.264 only.
config.amd_enforce_hrd_desc: Increases the constraints on rate control to meet HRD model requirements. This greatly reduces bitrate overflows, but may cause encoding artifacts or reduced quality on certain cards.
config.amd_preanalysis_desc: This enables rate-control preanalysis, which may increase quality at the expense of increased encoding latency.
config.amd_quality_desc: This controls the balance between encoding speed and quality.
config.amd_rc_cbr: cbr -- constant bitrate (recommended if HRD is enabled)
config.amd_rc_desc: This controls the rate control method to ensure we are not exceeding the client bitrate target. 'cqp' is not suitable for bitrate targeting, and other options besides 'vbr_latency' depend on HRD Enforcement to help constrain bitrate overflows.
config.amd_rc_vbr_latency: vbr_latency -- latency constrained variable bitrate (recommended if HRD is disabled; default)
config.amd_usage_desc: This sets the base encoding profile. All options presented below will override a subset of the usage profile, but there are additional hidden settings applied that cannot be configured elsewhere.
config.amd_vbaq_desc: The human visual system is typically less sensitive to artifacts in highly textured areas. In VBAQ mode, pixel variance is used to indicate the complexity of spatial textures, allowing the encoder to allocate more bits to smoother areas. Enabling this feature leads to improvements in subjective visual quality with some content.
config.apply_note: Click 'Apply' to restart Vibepollo and apply changes. This will terminate any running sessions.
config.audio_sink_desc_linux: The name of the audio sink used for Audio Loopback. If you do not specify this variable, pulseaudio will select the default monitor device. You can find the name of the audio sink using either command:
config.audio_sink_desc_macos: The name of the audio sink used for Audio Loopback. Vibepollo can only access microphones on macOS due to system limitations. To stream system audio using Soundflower or BlackHole.
config.audio_sink_desc_windows: Manually specify a specific audio device to capture. If unset, the device is chosen automatically. We strongly recommend leaving this field blank to use automatic device selection! If you have multiple audio devices with identical names, you can get the Device ID using the following command:
config.av1_mode_0: Vibepollo will advertise support for AV1 based on encoder capabilities (recommended)
config.av1_mode_1: Vibepollo will not advertise support for AV1
config.av1_mode_2: Vibepollo will advertise support for AV1 Main 8-bit profile
config.av1_mode_3: Vibepollo will advertise support for AV1 Main 8-bit and 10-bit (HDR) profiles
config.av1_mode_desc: Allows the client to request AV1 Main 8-bit or 10-bit video streams. AV1 is more CPU-intensive to encode, so enabling this may reduce performance when using software encoding.
config.prefer_10bit_sdr_desc: Capture and encode SDR sessions in 10-bit when the client and encoder negotiated HEVC/AV1 Main10 support (HDR stays off). Warning: May cause crashes on client devices with older GPUs that don't support HEVC 10-bit decoding.
config.back_button_timeout_desc: If the Back/Select button is held down for the specified number of milliseconds, a Home/Guide button press is emulated. If set to a value < 0 (default), holding the Back/Select button will not emulate the Home/Guide button.
config.capture_desc: On automatic mode Vibepollo will use the first one that works. NvFBC requires patched nvidia drivers.
config.lossless_scaling_legacy_auto_detect_desc: Uses a timer-based auto-apply flow instead of the Lossless Scaling hotkey. Not recommended unless the hotkey method fails, because detection can be less reliable.
config.cert_desc: The certificate used for the web UI and Moonlight client pairing. For best compatibility, this should have an RSA-2048 public key.
config.channels_desc_1: Vibepollo can allow a single streaming session to be shared with multiple clients simultaneously.
config.channels_desc_2: Some hardware encoders may have limitations that reduce performance with multiple streams.
config.coder_cabac: cabac -- context adaptive binary arithmetic coding - higher quality
config.coder_cavlc: cavlc -- context adaptive variable-length coding - faster decode
config.controller_desc: Allows guests to control the host system with a gamepad / controller
config.credentials_file_desc: Store Username/Password separately from Vibepollo's state file.
config.dd_config_ensure_only_display: Deactivate other displays and activate only the specified display
config.dd_config_ensure_primary: Activate the display automatically and make it a primary display
config.dd_config_revert_delay_desc: Additional delay in milliseconds to wait before reverting configuration when the app has been closed or the last session terminated. Main purpose is to provide a smoother transition when quickly switching between apps.
config.dd_config_revert_on_disconnect_desc: Revert configuration upon disconnect of all clients instead of app close or last session termination.
config.dd_paused_virtual_display_timeout_secs_d: Optional timeout while a session is paused and revert-on-disconnect is disabled. 0 keeps current behavior (no timeout).
config.dd_paused_virtual_display_timeout_secs_w: Removing a virtual display while a game is still running can cause crashes regardless of trigger (timeout, hotkey, or manual restore). This option helps prevent returning to a stuck virtual screen after inactivity.
config.dd_paused_virtual_display_timeout_secs_h: Alternative: use the restore hotkey to immediately terminate and restore display state when needed.
config.dd_snapshot_exclude_desc: Exclude physical dummy plugs from golden/session snapshots. Helpful when using both a virtual display and a physical dummy plug so restores avoid the plug.
config.dd_snapshot_exclude_placeholder: Choose devices (e.g., physical dummy plug) to exclude
config.dd_snapshot_exclude_warning: Keep at least one display unexcluded.
config.dd_always_restore_from_golden_desc: When enabled, the saved snapshot above is used first during display restore (reduces risk of stuck virtual screens). If the snapshot fails, falls back to session-based restore. Leave off unless you experience stuck displays.
config.golden_layout_upgrade_title: Golden display snapshot needs an update
config.golden_layout_upgrade_desc: Your saved snapshot predates display layout support. Recreate it to restore portrait and landscape monitor layouts correctly.
config.dd_config_verify_only: Verify that the display is enabled (default)
config.dd_hdr_option_auto: Switch on/off the HDR mode as requested by the client (default)
config.dd_wa_dummy_plug_hdr10: HDR 10-bit workaround for physical dummy plugs
config.dd_wa_dummy_plug_hdr10_desc: Locks physical dummy plugs to 30 Hz and keeps the VSYNC override active so HDR10 is recognized. Only enable this when you are using a hardware dummy plug. Applied to game launches only (Desktop streams keep their normal refresh rate).
config.dd_mode_remapping_desc_1: Keep the Windows desktop at a different resolution or refresh than the stream. Map what the client asks for to the mode you want on the host.
config.dd_mode_remapping_desc_2: The list is iterated from top to bottom and the first match is used.
config.dd_mode_remapping_desc_3: "Stream" fields can be left empty to match any requested value.
config.dd_mode_remapping_desc_example: Example: stream 1920×1080 to the client while keeping the desktop at 3840×2160 for the monitor connected to the host PC.
config.dd_mode_remapping_desc_4_final_values_mi: At least one "Final" field must be specified. The unspecified resolution or refresh rate will not be changed.
config.dd_mode_remapping_desc_4_final_values_no: "Final" field must be specified and cannot be empty.
config.dd_display_setup_intro: Choose the display to capture and how Vibepollo should prepare Windows just before a stream starts (turn on a monitor, switch primary, set resolution/refresh rate, HDR).
config.dd_automation_desc: Automatically adjust the host display to match the client's requested resolution and refresh rate before streaming.
config.app_virtual_display_mode_hint: When this app runs on the Vibepollo virtual display, choose whether to use a per-client or shared virtual screen and configure the layout below. Reset to follow the global display configuration.
config.app_virtual_display_layout_follow_global: Following the global virtual display layout.
config.app_display_override_hint: Choose whether this app should launch on the Vibepollo virtual display or target a specific physical monitor instead of the global default.
config.app_display_physical_hint: Pick the monitor to use when launching this app. Physical dummy plugs show up here even if they are inactive.
config.app_display_physical_status_hint: Clear the selection to follow the global display setting for this app.
config.dd_pre_stream_intro: Vibepollo applies these settings automatically when starting a stream. Use them to activate a display, make it primary, and optionally adjust resolution, refresh rate, and HDR.
config.dd_config_hint: Choose how Vibepollo prepares the display before streaming.
config.dd_activate_virtual_display_desc: When using Vibepollo's virtual display driver, turn the virtual monitor on and temporarily make it the only active display while a stream is starting.
config.dd_refresh_rate_option_auto: Use FPS value provided by the client (default)
config.dd_refresh_rate_option_manual_desc: ⚠️ Enforcing a manual refresh rate forces the host to this refresh for all streams, overriding any refresh rules below and the "Double refresh rate for virtual displays" option.
config.dd_resolution_option_auto: Use resolution provided by the client (default)
config.dd_wa_virtual_double_refresh: Double refresh rate for virtual displays
config.dd_wa_virtual_double_refresh_desc: Creates Vibepollo virtual displays at 2x the target refresh to prevent unexplained FPS drops. Leave this on unless a specific game or monitor behaves incorrectly. Note: Manual refresh rate overrides this (no doubling).
config.dd_wa_hdr_toggle_delay_desc_1: When using virtual display device (VDD) for streaming, it might incorrectly display HDR color. Vibepollo can try to mitigate this issue, by turning HDR off and then on again.
config.dd_wa_hdr_toggle_delay_desc_2: If the value is set to 0, the workaround is disabled (default). If the value is between 0 and 3000 milliseconds, Vibepollo will turn off HDR, wait for the specified amount of time and then turn HDR on again. The recommended delay time is around 500 milliseconds in most cases.
config.dd_wa_hdr_toggle_delay_desc_3: DO NOT use this workaround unless you actually have issues with HDR as it directly impacts stream start time!
config.dd_wa_hdr_toggle_desc: When using a virtual display device (VDD), HDR colors can be wrong. Vibepollo can toggle HDR off and back on to mitigate this. Enable only if you see HDR color issues.
config.ds4_back_as_touchpad_click_desc: When forcing DS4 emulation, map Back/Select to Touchpad Click
config.ds5_inputtino_randomize_mac_desc: Upon controller registration use a random MAC instead of one based on the controllers internal index to avoid mixing configuration settings of different controllers when the are swapped on client-side.
config.encoder_desc: Force a specific encoder, otherwise Vibepollo will select the best available option. Note: If you specify a hardware encoder on Windows, it must match the GPU where the display is connected.
config.external_ip_desc: If no external IP address is given, Vibepollo will automatically detect external IP
config.fec_percentage_desc: Percentage of error correcting packets per data packet in each video frame. Higher values can correct for more network packet loss, but at the cost of increasing bandwidth usage.
config.frame_limiter_fps_limit_desc: Optional global FPS limit for the frame limiter. Set to 0 to use the stream's requested FPS.
config.file_apps_desc: The file where current apps of Vibepollo are stored.
config.file_state_desc: The file where current state of Vibepollo is stored
config.gamepad_desc: Choose which type of gamepad to emulate on the host
config.global_prep_cmd_desc: Configure a list of commands to be executed before or after running any application. If any of the specified preparation commands fail, the application launch process will be aborted.
config.hevc_mode_0: Vibepollo will advertise support for HEVC based on encoder capabilities (recommended)
config.hevc_mode_1: Vibepollo will not advertise support for HEVC
config.hevc_mode_2: Vibepollo will advertise support for HEVC Main profile
config.hevc_mode_3: Vibepollo will advertise support for HEVC Main and Main10 (HDR) profiles
config.hevc_mode_desc: Allows the client to request HEVC Main or HEVC Main10 video streams. HEVC is more CPU-intensive to encode, so enabling this may reduce performance when using software encoding.
config.high_resolution_scrolling_desc: When enabled, Vibepollo will pass through high resolution scroll events from Moonlight clients. This can be useful to disable for older applications that scroll too fast with high resolution scroll events.
config.install_steam_audio_drivers_desc: If Steam is installed, this will automatically install the Steam Streaming Speakers driver to support 5.1/7.1 surround sound and muting host audio.
config.key_repeat_delay_desc: Control how fast keys will repeat themselves. The initial delay in milliseconds before repeating keys.
config.key_repeat_frequency_desc: How often keys repeat every second. This configurable option supports decimals.
config.key_rightalt_to_key_win: Map Right Alt key to Windows key
config.key_rightalt_to_key_win_desc: It may be possible that you cannot send the Windows Key from Moonlight directly. In those cases it may be useful to make Vibepollo think the Right Alt key is the Windows key
config.keyboard_desc: Allows guests to control the host system with the keyboard
config.lan_encryption_mode_desc: This determines when encryption will be used when streaming over your local network. Encryption can reduce streaming performance, particularly on less powerful hosts and clients.
config.locale_desc: The locale used for Vibepollo's user interface.
config.log_path_desc: The file where the current logs of Vibepollo are stored.
config.max_bitrate_desc: The maximum bitrate (in Kbps) that Vibepollo will encode the stream at. If set to 0, it will always use the bitrate requested by Moonlight.
config.minimum_fps_target_desc: The lowest effective FPS a stream can reach. A value of 0 is treated as roughly half of the stream's FPS. A setting of 20 is recommended if you stream 24 or 30fps content.
config.session_token_ttl_seconds_desc: How long a Web UI login session remains valid before requiring re-authentication. Reduce for higher security; increase for convenience.
config.remember_me_refresh_token_ttl_seconds_de: How long a remembered device stays trusted before requiring sign-in again. Use a lower value for higher security.
config.min_log_level_desc: The minimum log level printed to standard out
config.min_threads_desc: Increasing the value slightly reduces encoding efficiency, but the tradeoff is usually worth it to gain the use of more CPU cores for encoding. The ideal value is the lowest value that can reliably encode at your desired streaming settings on your hardware.
config.motion_as_ds4: Emulate a DS4 gamepad if the client gamepad reports motion sensors are present
config.motion_as_ds4_desc: If disabled, motion sensors will not be taken into account during gamepad type selection.
config.mouse_desc: Allows guests to control the host system with the mouse
config.native_pen_touch_desc: When enabled, Vibepollo will pass through native pen/touch events from Moonlight clients. This can be useful to disable for older applications without native pen/touch support.
config.notify_pre_releases_desc: Whether to be notified of new pre-release versions of Vibepollo
config.nvenc_h264_cavlc: Prefer CAVLC over CABAC in H.264
config.nvenc_h264_cavlc_desc: Simpler form of entropy coding. CAVLC needs around 10% more bitrate for same quality. Only relevant for really old decoding devices.
config.nvenc_latency_over_power: Prefer lower encoding latency over power savings
config.nvenc_latency_over_power_desc: Vibepollo requests maximum GPU clock speed while streaming to reduce encoding latency. Disabling it is not recommended since this can lead to significantly increased encoding latency.
config.nvenc_opengl_vulkan_on_dxgi: Present OpenGL/Vulkan on top of DXGI
config.nvenc_opengl_vulkan_on_dxgi_desc: Vibepollo can't capture fullscreen OpenGL and Vulkan programs at full frame rate unless they present on top of DXGI. This is system-wide setting that is reverted on sunshine program exit.
config.nvenc_preset_desc: Higher numbers improve compression (quality at given bitrate) at the cost of increased encoding latency. Recommended to change only when limited by network or decoder, otherwise similar effect can be accomplished by increasing bitrate.
config.nvenc_realtime_hags: Use realtime priority in hardware accelerated gpu scheduling
config.nvenc_realtime_hags_desc: Currently NVIDIA drivers may freeze in encoder when HAGS is enabled, realtime priority is used and VRAM utilization is close to maximum. Disabling this option lowers the priority to high, sidestepping the freeze at the cost of reduced capture performance when the GPU is heavily loaded.
config.nvenc_spatial_aq_desc: Assign higher QP values to flat regions of the video. Recommended to enable when streaming at lower bitrates.
config.nvenc_force_split_encode_desc: NVIDIA drivers already enable split-frame encoding automatically for 4K and higher resolutions. Enable this to force it on for lower resolutions too, such as 1440p at 120 Hz, when using NVENC HEVC or AV1.
config.nvenc_twopass_desc: Adds preliminary encoding pass. This allows to detect more motion vectors, better distribute bitrate across the frame and more strictly adhere to bitrate limits. Disabling it is not recommended since this can lead to occasional bitrate overshoot and subsequent packet loss.
config.nvenc_vbv_increase_desc: By default sunshine uses single-frame VBV/HRD, which means any encoded video frame size is not expected to exceed requested bitrate divided by requested frame rate. Relaxing this restriction can be beneficial and act as low-latency variable bitrate, but may also lead to packet loss if the network doesn't have buffer headroom to handle bitrate spikes. Maximum accepted value is 400, which corresponds to 5x increased encoded video frame upper size limit.
config.origin_web_ui_allowed_desc: The origin of the remote endpoint address that is not denied access to Web UI
config.origin_web_ui_allowed_lan: Only those in LAN may access Web UI
config.origin_web_ui_allowed_pc: Only localhost may access Web UI
config.output_name_desc_unix: During Vibepollo startup, you should see the list of detected displays. Note: You need to use the id value inside the parenthesis. Below is an example; the actual output can be found in the Troubleshooting tab.
config.output_name_desc_windows: Manually specify a display device id to use for capture. If unset, the primary display is captured. Note: If you specified a GPU above, this display must be connected to that GPU. During Vibepollo startup, you should see the list of detected displays. Below is an example; the actual output can be found in the Troubleshooting tab.
config.ping_timeout_desc: How long to wait in milliseconds for data from moonlight before shutting down the stream
config.pkey_desc: The private key used for the web UI and Moonlight client pairing. For best compatibility, this should be an RSA-2048 private key.
config.port_desc: Set the family of ports used by Vibepollo
config.port_http_port_note: Use this port to connect with Moonlight.
config.port_warning: Exposing the Web UI to the internet is a security risk! Proceed at your own risk!
config.qp_desc: Some devices may not support Constant Bit Rate. For those devices, QP is used instead. Higher value means more compression, but less quality.
config.qsv_slow_hevc_desc: This can enable HEVC encoding on older Intel GPUs, at the cost of higher GPU usage and worse performance.
config.restart_note: Vibepollo is restarting to apply changes.
config.stream_audio_desc: Whether to stream audio or not. Disabling this can be useful for streaming headless displays as second monitors.
config.sunshine_name_desc: The name displayed by Moonlight. If not specified, the PC's hostname is used
config.sw_preset_desc: Optimize the trade-off between encoding speed (encoded frames per second) and compression efficiency (quality per bit in the bitstream). Defaults to superfast.
config.sw_tune_animation: animation -- good for cartoons; uses higher deblocking and more reference frames
config.sw_tune_desc: Tuning options, which are applied after the preset. Defaults to zerolatency.
config.sw_tune_fastdecode: fastdecode -- allows faster decoding by disabling certain filters
config.sw_tune_film: film -- use for high quality movie content; lowers deblocking
config.sw_tune_grain: grain -- preserves the grain structure in old, grainy film material
config.sw_tune_zerolatency: zerolatency -- good for fast encoding and low-latency streaming (default)
config.system_tray_desc: Show icon in system tray and display desktop notifications
config.touchpad_as_ds4: Emulate a DS4 gamepad if the client gamepad reports a touchpad is present
config.touchpad_as_ds4_desc: If disabled, touchpad presence will not be taken into account during gamepad type selection.
config.upnp_desc: Automatically configure port forwarding for streaming over the Internet
config.vaapi_strict_rc_buffer: Strictly enforce frame bitrate limits for H.264/HEVC on AMD GPUs
config.vaapi_strict_rc_buffer_desc: Enabling this option can avoid dropped frames over the network during scene changes, but video quality may be reduced during motion.
config.virtual_sink_desc: Manually specify a virtual audio device to use. If unset, the device is chosen automatically. We strongly recommend leaving this field blank to use automatic device selection!
config.wan_encryption_mode_desc: This determines when encryption will be used when streaming over the Internet. Encryption can reduce streaming performance, particularly on less powerful hosts and clients.
config.dd_config_ensure_active_warning: Windows does not display the lock screen on external monitors until you click on them, which can cause black screens when streaming. If this is an issue, use the primary display option instead.
config.dd_snapshot_restore_hotkey_desc: Windows-only global hotkey. Useful for forcing virtual displays off and restoring snapshots when Vibepollo is paused or stuck. Click the field and press the combo you want.
config.dd_snapshot_restore_hotkey_invalid: Unsupported key. Use F1-F24, letters, or digits.
config.dd_snapshot_restore_hotkey_modifiers_des: Modifiers for the restore hotkey (e.g., ctrl+alt+shift, ctrl|shift, win, none). Supported tokens: ctrl/control, alt, shift, win/windows/meta, none/off/disabled.
config.virtual_display_mode_step_hint: Select which display Vibepollo should prepare: keep your physical monitor, create a per-client virtual display, or reuse a shared virtual screen.
config.virtual_display_hdr_tip: HDR Tip: Run Windows HDR Calibration while streaming and save the profile. Then open the Clients tab and click HDR Color Profile for the client to choose the saved profile.
config.virtual_display_layout_hint: Choose how Vibepollo positions and prioritizes your virtual display when it is enabled.
config.virtual_display_toggle_hint: Override the display Vibepollo uses for this app: switch to the Vibepollo virtual display or pick a specific physical monitor. Leave unchecked to follow the global/client choice.
config.client_display_override_hint: Override how Vibepollo chooses a display for this client. Leave unchecked to follow the global display settings.
config.virtual_display_layout_exclusive_desc: Disable every other monitor so only the Vibepollo virtual display stays visible.
config.virtual_display_layout_extended_desc: Keep your physical monitors active and simply add the virtual display as another screen.
config.virtual_display_layout_extended_primary_: Extend the desktop and make the virtual display the primary monitor (taskbar, login, etc.).
config.virtual_display_layout_extended_isolated: Extend the desktop but park the virtual display far away so the mouse never wanders into it.
config.virtual_display_layout_extended_primary_: Combine primary + isolation: make the virtual display primary while keeping it out of reach of physical monitors.
config.virtual_display_status_hint: Use Vibepollo's virtual display to spin up a high-refresh virtual monitor automatically. If the driver reports an error, reinstall Vibepollo or repair the driver before streaming.
config.dd_resolution_option_manual_desc: ⚠️ Enforcing a manual resolution disables all display overrides below. The host will always be set to this resolution regardless of any override rules.
config.vigem_missing_desc: Vibepollo requires the ViGEmBus driver to emulate controllers on Windows. It is no longer bundled. Please download and install it manually:
config.crash_dump_desc: Vibepollo detected a crash dump within the last week. Please export a crash bundle and include it when opening an issue.
config.update_check_interval_desc: How often Vibepollo should check for updates automatically (based on git tags). Set 0 to disable periodic checks.
config.hot_deferred: Settings saved. Changes will apply when the current stream ends (or on next start).
config.saved_deferred: Changes will apply on next stream
config.audio_sink_desc_unix: The name of the audio sink used for Audio Loopback. If you do not specify this, the system default is used. On Linux, pulseaudio selects the default monitor device; on macOS, use a loopback device such as Soundflower or BlackHole.
config.nvenc_section_desc: Advanced NVIDIA NVENC tuning options. Change only if you need to address specific encoder behavior or performance issues.
config.nvenc_intra_refresh_desc: Use gradual intra refresh instead of full IDR frames to smooth bitrate spikes. Disable if your client has decoder issues.
config.global_state_cmd_desc: Configure commands that run when the stream state changes. These are saved with the rest of the server configuration and require a manual save.
config.server_cmd_desc: Configure a list of commands to be executed when called from client during streaming.
config.enable_pairing_desc: Enable pairing for the Moonlight client. This allows the client to authenticate with the host and establish a secure connection.
config.enable_discovery_desc: When disabled, you'll need to manually enter host IP on the client to pair.
config.enable_input_only_mode_desc: Add an Input Only app entry. When enabled, the app list will only show the current running app and the Input Only entry when streaming. The Input Only entry will not receive any image or audio. Useful for operating the desktop on TV or connecting peripherals which the TV doesn't support with a phone.
config.hide_tray_controls_desc: Do not show "Force Stop", "Restart" and "Quit" in tray menu.
config.fallback_mode_desc: Vibepollo will use this mode when the client does not provide a mode or when the app is launched through the web UI. Format: [Width]x[Height]x[FPS]
config.keep_sink_default_desc: Leave the virtual audio sink selected as the default playback device while streaming audio is active.
config.auto_capture_sink_desc: Automatically follow the current default audio sink instead of sticking to the originally selected device.
config.limit_framerate_desc: Limit the framerate being captured to client requested framerate. May not run at full framerate if vsync is enabled and display refreshrate does not match requested framerate. Could cause lag on some clients if disabled.
config.envvar_compatibility_mode_desc: Enable compatibility mode for environment variables. This will modify the behavior of certain environment variables to be more compatible with older tools.
config.ignore_encoder_probe_failure_desc: Allow streaming to continue even if probing for encoders fails. This may result in streaming failure if no encoder is available.
config.legacy_ordering_desc: Enable ordering support workaround for legacy clients. Can cause issues with clients or scripts that can't handle UTF8 correctly. Artemis clients support this by default.

index.description: Vibepollo is a self-hosted game stream host for Moonlight.
index.installed_version_not_stable: You are running a pre-release version of Vibepollo. You may experience bugs or other issues. Please report any issues you encounter. Thank you for helping to make Vibepollo a better software!
index.new_pre_release: A new Pre-Release Version is Available!
index.new_stable: A new Stable Version is Available!
index.startup_errors: Attention! Vibepollo detected these errors during startup. We STRONGLY RECOMMEND fixing them before streaming.
index.version_dirty: Thank you for helping to make Vibepollo a better software!
index.version_latest: You are running the latest version of Vibepollo
index.version_ahead: You are running a development build ahead of the latest release ({ahead} commits)
index.version_behind: Your build is {behind} commits behind the latest release; consider updating.
index.version_compare_unknown: Unable to determine commit distance to latest release.

password.new_username_desc: If not specified, the username will not change
password.success_msg: Password has been changed successfully! This page will reload soon, your browser will ask you for the new credentials.

pin.pair_failure: Pairing Failed: Check if the PIN is typed correctly
pin.pair_success: Success! Please check Moonlight to continue
pin.warning_msg: Make sure you have access to the client you are pairing with. This software can give total control to your computer, so be careful!
pin.display_mode_override_desc: Force Vibepollo to use this resolution and refresh rate for this client (format: 1920x1080x60). Leave blank to use the client's request.

resource_card.legal_desc: By continuing to use this software you agree to the terms and conditions in the following documents.

troubleshooting.dd_reset_desc: If Vibepollo is stuck trying to restore the changed display device settings, you can reset the settings and proceed to restore the display state manually.
troubleshooting.dd_export_golden_desc: Create a backup of your current Windows display layout. This backup is used to safely restore your monitor if Windows doesn’t keep your settings.
troubleshooting.dd_export_golden_error: Failed to export golden display snapshot.
troubleshooting.dd_golden_help: Save a snapshot of your current Windows display layout (which screens are on, primary display, resolution, HDR). Vibepollo uses this if your monitor settings don’t stick — for example after a reboot or at the sign‑in screen. This reduces the chance of coming back to a PC with the primary display still turned off.
troubleshooting.dd_golden_delete_error: Failed to delete golden display snapshot.
troubleshooting.force_close_desc: If Moonlight complains about an app currently running, force closing the app should fix the issue.
troubleshooting.logs_desc: See the logs uploaded by Vibepollo
troubleshooting.restart_sunshine_desc: If Vibepollo isn't working properly, you can try restarting it. This will terminate any running sessions.
troubleshooting.unpair_desc: Remove your paired devices. Individually unpaired devices with an active session will remain connected, but cannot start or resume a session.
troubleshooting.unpair_single_success: However, the device(s) may still be in an active session. Use the 'Force Close' button above to end any open sessions.
troubleshooting.new_logs_available: New logs available — jump to latest
troubleshooting.collect_playnite_logs_desc: Export Vibepollo log, Playnite/plugin logs, and display-helper logs.
troubleshooting.export_crash_bundle_desc: Download logs along with the most recent Vibepollo crash dump.

welcome.create_creds: Before Getting Started, we need you to make a new username and password for accessing the Web UI.
welcome.create_creds_alert: The credentials below are needed to access Vibepollo's Web UI. Keep them safe, since you will never see them again!
welcome.welcome_success: This page will reload soon, your browser will ask you for the new credentials

rtss.desc: Vibepollo can apply a global frame limit via RTSS (recommended) or NVIDIA Control Panel (experimental), then restores the original driver settings when streaming stops.
rtss.frame_limiter_disable_vsync_desc: When enabled, Vibepollo sets the NVIDIA driver VSYNC setting to Off for the duration of the stream and restores the previous value afterward. If NVIDIA overrides are unavailable, Vibepollo forces the display to run at its highest refresh rate as a best-effort fallback.
rtss.frame_limiter_fps_limit_desc: Optional global FPS limit for the frame limiter. Set to 0 to use the stream's requested FPS.
rtss.provider_desc: Auto prefers RTSS whenever it is detected and falls back to the NVIDIA Control Panel limiter if needed. NVIDIA's limiter is not recommended because it cannot guarantee perfect frame pacing.
rtss.status_unknown: Unable to determine frame limiter status
rtss.install_path_desc: Root install folder (leave blank to auto-detect under Program Files / Program Files (x86)).
rtss.enable_frame_limiter_desc: When enabled, Vibepollo applies a global frame limit using your chosen provider for the duration of each stream, then restores the previous driver setting afterward.
rtss.sync_limiter_desc: Advanced users only. Vibepollo mirrors this into RTSS. See the comparison below before changing it.
rtss.sync_limiter_help_blurb: Async is recommended. It adds a small amount of latency but all but removes microstuttering, which is usually more noticeable to viewers.
rtss.sync_limiter_async_use: Most use cases where smoothness matters more than absolute latency
rtss.sync_limiter_front_use: Latency-critical sessions that can tolerate occasional judder
rtss.sync_limiter_back_stutter: Reduced tearing but stutter when frametimes spike
rtss.sync_limiter_back_use: Displays that behave better with vsync-style pacing
rtss.sync_limiter_reflex_use: Only for testing when Reflex is mandatory
rtss.recommend_copy: RTSS delivers the smoothest streaming experience. Install it if missing; NVIDIA's frame limiter is not recommended because it cannot guarantee perfect frame pacing.
rtss.status_bootstrap: RTSS detected; Vibepollo will refresh RTSS configuration on the next stream
rtss.status_bootstrap_hint: Vibepollo manages RTSS configuration automatically; no action needed.
rtss.status_autolaunch_hint: Vibepollo will launch RTSS automatically when streaming starts.
rtss.sync_limiter_async_advantages: Stable timing regardless of refresh alignment; very smooth at odd FPS or Hz combos
rtss.sync_limiter_async_disadvantages: Slightly higher latency than Front Edge or Reflex
rtss.sync_limiter_front_advantages: Absolute lowest latency if FPS divides evenly into refresh; required for Scanline Sync
rtss.sync_limiter_front_disadvantages: Adds latency when CPU-bound because finished frames wait; fragile pacing at mismatched caps
rtss.sync_limiter_back_advantages: Scan-aligned pacing, smoother than Front Edge at non-divisor FPS
rtss.sync_limiter_back_disadvantages: Slightly higher latency; not as low as Reflex or Async
rtss.sync_limiter_reflex_advantages: Can reduce latency in Reflex-optimized titles; integrates with DLSS 3 Frame Generation
rtss.sync_limiter_reflex_disadvantages: Requires explicit game support; inconsistent behavior; microstutter issues are common

frameLimiter.enableHint: Vibepollo applies the selected limiter provider at stream start and restores the previous driver setting when the stream ends.
frameLimiter.providerHint: Auto prefers RTSS whenever it is available and falls back to the NVIDIA Control Panel limiter if needed. NVIDIA's limiter is not recommended because it cannot guarantee perfect frame pacing.
frameLimiter.limitHint: Optional global FPS limit for NVIDIA or RTSS. Set to 0 to use the stream's requested FPS.
frameLimiter.vsyncUllmForcedByDummyPlug: Forced on because the dummy plug HDR workaround requires VSYNC to be off for 10-bit output.
frameLimiter.vsyncUllmHintNv: Vibepollo will force the NVIDIA driver VSYNC setting to Off during the stream and restore it afterward. Use this if you globally enable VSYNC but want Vibepollo sessions without it.
frameLimiter.vsyncUllmHintGeneric: Vibepollo will run the display at its highest available refresh rate during the stream as a best-effort VSYNC workaround. NVIDIA overrides are not available on this system.
frameLimiter.rtssPathHint: Root install folder (leave blank to auto-detect under Program Files / Program Files (x86)).
frameLimiter.syncLimiterHint: Optional: adjust RTSS SyncLimiter when Vibepollo uses RTSS to limit framerate.
frameLimiter.rtssMissing: RTSS was not detected. Install RTSS or adjust the install path to use it as a limiter.

status.error: Failed to query frame limiter status.
status.rtssBootstrap: RTSS detected; Vibepollo will refresh RTSS configuration when streaming starts.
status.rtssBootstrapHint: Vibepollo manages RTSS configuration automatically; no action needed.
status.rtssAutolaunchHint: Vibepollo will launch RTSS automatically when a stream begins.

frameLimiter.noticeCopy: RTSS offers the most stable frame pacing. Vibepollo will use it automatically when available; NVIDIA's limiter is not recommended because it cannot guarantee perfect frame pacing.

offline.title: Lost Connection to Vibepollo
offline.description: The web interface lost connectivity to the Vibepollo service.
offline.retrying: Attempting to reconnect… The page will refresh once available.
offline.close_hint: If you stopped Vibepollo intentionally, you may close this tab.
offline.output_name_desc_unix: During Vibepollo startup, you should see the list of detected displays. Note: You need to use the id value inside the parenthesis. Below is an example; the actual output can be found in the Troubleshooting tab.
offline.output_name_desc_windows: Manually specify a display device id to use for capture. If unset, the primary display is captured. Note: If you specified a GPU above, this display must be connected to that GPU. During Vibepollo startup, you should see the list of detected displays. Below is an example; the actual output can be found in the Troubleshooting tab.

webrtc.title: WebRTC Streaming
webrtc.subtitle: Experimental browser streaming with higher latency and lower quality than native streaming.
webrtc.experimental_notice: Experimental: expect higher latency and lower quality than native streaming.
webrtc.client_profile_hint: Save stream settings under a client name to reuse them later.
webrtc.frame_pacing_desc: Balance latency and smoothness by delaying early frames and dropping late ones.
webrtc.hdr_desc: Requires HEVC or AV1 and an HDR-capable browser/decoder. Enabling HDR may switch codecs.
webrtc.hdr_unavailable: HDR isn't available in this browser.
webrtc.mute_host_audio_desc: Silences audio playback on the host PC during streaming.
webrtc.idle_game_selected: Press Start to launch the selected game
webrtc.idle_no_selection: Select a game or stream the desktop
webrtc.no_selection: No game selected - will resume current session
webrtc.terminate_desc: Ends streaming and closes the running app.
webrtc.terminate_confirm_message: A session is already running. Starting {app} will terminate the current session. Continue?

clients.title: Client Management
clients.pair_desc: Enter the 4-digit PIN shown in Moonlight along with a friendly device name to pair this client.
clients.hdr_profile_desc: Install and run the Windows HDR Calibration app from the Microsoft Store while this client is streaming, save the profile, then come back here (Clients tab → HDR Color Profile) and select the profile you saved. Profiles are loaded from the system color profile directory and sorted by newest first.
clients.hdr_profile_load_failed: Failed to load HDR color profiles.
clients.confirm_remove_message: Are you sure you want to remove this client? It will need to be re-paired.
clients.confirm_remove_message_named: This will unpair {name} from Vibepollo. They will need to pair again to connect.
clients.confirm_unpair_all_message: Are you sure you want to unpair all clients? They will all need to be re-paired.
clients.confirm_unpair_all_message_count: This will unpair all {count} client(s). They will all need to pair again to connect.

playnite.title: Playnite Integration
playnite.desc: Vibepollo can sync your Playnite library so your installed games appear automatically. First, install Playnite, run it, and configure it to start with Windows. Then install the Vibepollo plugin below to enable the integration.
playnite.only_windows: Playnite integration is available on Windows only.
playnite.limited_tooltip: Games cannot be auto-synced while Playnite is closed. Existing games added to Vibepollo will still work. Once you launch Playnite, syncing resumes automatically.
playnite.status_not_running_unknown: Playnite not running — plugin status unknown
playnite.selects_disabled_hint: Cannot modify without Playnite connectivity. Start Playnite to enable this.
playnite.uninstall_requires_restart: Uninstalling the Playnite plugin may require restarting Playnite. Continue?
playnite.install_requires_restart: Installing or updating the Playnite plugin requires restarting Playnite. Continue?
playnite.plugin_outdated: A newer Playnite plugin is available. Installed: v{installed}, Latest: v{latest}.
playnite.sync_all_installed_desc: Adds every installed Playnite game to Vibepollo, alongside recent and category selections.
playnite.enable_autosync_hint: Enable synchronization to edit these settings.
playnite.recent_games_desc: Maximum number of recent games to auto-sync. 0 = disable recent syncing.
playnite.recent_max_age_days_desc: A game counts as recent only if played in the last N days. 0 = ignore play date.
playnite.delete_after_days_desc: Remove auto-synced games that haven’t been launched within N days of being added. 0 = never remove.
playnite.policy_prune_immediately: Always prune games that no longer qualify (may leave empty slots)
playnite.policy_explainer: Choose how Vibepollo removes old auto-synced games.
playnite.sync_categories_help: Pick Playnite categories sync, which will add all games in that category regardless if they are recently played or not. This will not consume any recently played slot counts, it is simply appended to them.
playnite.sync_plugins_help: Auto-sync every installed game provided by the selected library plugins.
playnite.exclude_categories_help: Games tagged with these categories will never be auto-synced.
playnite.exclude_plugins_help: Games imported from these plugins will never be auto-synced.
playnite.remove_uninstalled_desc: When enabled, Vibepollo removes auto-synced games as soon as they are uninstalled in Playnite.
playnite.focus_attempts_help: Times to try bringing Playnite windows to foreground when launching.
playnite.focus_timeout_secs_help: How long auto-focus runs while re-applying focus (0 to disable).
playnite.focus_exit_on_first: Stop auto-focus after first successful focus
playnite.focus_exit_on_first_help: Stop auto-focus after the first success; otherwise, re-apply until attempts or timeout elapse.
playnite.diagnostic_not_installed: Playnite plugin is not installed in the Extensions directory.
playnite.diagnostic_not_running: Playnite is not running. Launch it to resume syncing.
playnite.exclude_games_desc: Selected games will not be auto-synced from Playnite.
playnite.add_exclusions_hint: Pick one or more games to exclude from auto-sync.
playnite.games_not_available: No games available. Start Playnite to fetch games.
playnite.games_unavailable_indicator: Cannot retrieve Playnite games right now. Start Playnite to load games.
playnite.games_cached_indicator: Showing cached Playnite games due to limited connectivity.
playnite.delete_autosync_body: This removes every Playnite-managed auto-sync entry from the Applications list. Apps added manually are not affected.
playnite.delete_autosync_error: Failed to delete auto-synced Playnite games.
playnite.summary_recent_limit: Up to {n} most-recently played games will be auto-synced.
playnite.summary_keep_until_replaced: Games stay until a newer game replaces them.
playnite.summary_prune_immediately: Games are pruned when they no longer qualify.
playnite.summary_all_installed: All installed Playnite games are kept in Vibepollo.
playnite.summary_plugin_include: Includes all games from {count} selected library plugins.
playnite.summary_remove_uninstalled_off: Uninstalled games remain until you remove them manually.
playnite.summary_delete_after: Also remove games never launched after {days} days.

auth.title: API Token Management
auth.token_success: Success! Copy this token now as you will not see it again:
auth.please_specify_scope: Please specify at least one path and corresponding method(s).
auth.confirm_revoke: Are you sure you want to revoke this token? This action cannot be undone.
auth.confirm_revoke_message_hash: Revoke token {hash}? This immediately disables the token and cannot be undone.
auth.select_api_path_and_token: Please select an API path and provide a token.
auth.remember_me_label: Stay signed in on this device
auth.generate_token_help: Select one or more API routes and allowed HTTP methods to embed in the new token.
auth.search_tokens: Search by hash, user, or path...
auth.generate_disabled_hint: Add at least one scope to generate a token.
auth.testing_help: Use this form to validate an existing token against a read-only endpoint.
auth.logout_success: You have been securely logged out.
auth.logout_refresh_hint: You may now close this tab or refresh the page to sign back in.
auth.sessions_description: Devices with active Vibepollo sessions. Remove entries to sign them out remotely.
auth.sessions_revoke_message: Are you sure you want to revoke access for {device}?
auth.noticeCopy: RTSS offers the most stable frame pacing. Vibepollo will use it automatically when available; NVIDIA's limiter is not recommended because it cannot guarantee perfect frame pacing.
